prompt:
Using REFERENCE_0 as the main character and composition base, and REFERENCE_1 only as mood inspiration, transform the photo into a single vertical Y2K retro magazine-cover poster in clean flat vector illustration. Keep the woman’s identity, pose, hairstyle, hair color, outfit, body proportions, and sunlit bedroom setting from REFERENCE_0 recognizable, but simplify everything into bold cel-shaded vector shapes with crisp dark outlines, smooth gradients, and stylized high-contrast shadows.

Goal: Create a cute Y2K magazine surface with a subtle cool melancholy edge.

Canvas and layout: Vertical poster cover, full-bleed, with a rounded rectangular border and graphic magazine framing. Place the character centered in the lower/middle area, cropped similarly to a fashion cover. Add a large headline at the top reading Y2K in oversized pink bubble letters with shiny chrome/silver 3D edging.

New graphic additions: Add exactly 2 glossy pink/blue ringed planets, 1 large planet on the left side and 1 smaller planet near the bottom-right. Add exactly 8 sparkle/star graphics distributed around the cover: one large chrome sparkle at top-left, one small sparkle near the left planet, one pink sparkle on the right, one blue sparkle on the right, one lime sparkle on the right, one white sparkle near the lower-right frame, one lime star near the lower-left, and one tiny white sparkle near the lower center-left. Add a lime orbital swoosh behind the headline, vertical decorative dot rows, a small barcode box at bottom-left, and a bottom-right oval badge reading POP with the smaller subtitle MELANCHOLY.

Style: 2000s pop magazine cover, playful pastel cyber aesthetic, flat vector fashion illustration, blue/purple background blocks, pink and lime accents, glossy plastic/chrome effects on typography and icons, clean poster-print finish. Preserve the warm sunlight-and-shadow feeling from the source photo but render it as graphic shapes.

Constraints: Do not change the woman into a different person, do not add extra characters, do not add unreadable filler cover lines, keep all text limited to the headline, badge subtitle, and barcode-style graphic.
